intro: Microcharis tritoides, also known as the African cat grass, is a plant species belonging to the Leguminosae family. Traditionally, it has been used in African folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Microcharis tritoides include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
